originalTitle: Senegal government alleges corruption over Afcon
title: Senegal government alleges corruption over Afcon
content: The Senegalese government calls for an "independent international investigation" at the Confederation of African Football after it stripped Senegal of the Africa Cup of Nations title.

originalTitle: Celebrations in Morocco but devastation in Senegal after Afcon decision
title: Celebrations in Morocco but devastation in Senegal after Afcon decision
content: Moroccans feel justice has been done but the Senegalese are angry after being stripped of the title.

originalTitle: 'The longest VAR check in history' - the fallout from shock Afcon decision
title: Our Big African Football Game Had a Long, Long Wait!
content:
Hello, Football Friends!

Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter here, ready to tell you about some exciting things happening in our wonderful Africa! Today, we're going to talk about football, also known as soccer! It's a game many of us love to watch and play. Imagine a big party, with lots of singing and dancing, but with a football!

What is AFCON? Our Big Party!

Africa has a super special football tournament! It's called AFCON. That's short for the Africa Cup of Nations. It's like a giant party where teams from all over Africa come together to play football. They want to find out who is the best team in Africa! It happens every two years, and everyone gets very excited. Did you know? AFCON is one of the oldest football tournaments in the world, starting way back in 1957!

The Big Game: A Final Match!

In AFCON, many teams play games. But at the very end, there are two super strong teams left. They play one last game called the "final match." This game is very important! The team that wins the final match gets a big trophy and becomes the champion of Africa. Everyone watches the final with big smiles and nervous hearts! Did you know? The trophy for AFCON is called the "Africa Cup." It's very shiny!

Meet the Referee and VAR!

In every football game, there's a special person called the referee. The referee is like the head teacher of the game! They make sure everyone plays fair and follows the rules. They wear a special uniform and have a whistle. If someone breaks a rule, the referee blows their whistle. But sometimes, even referees need a little help. That's where VAR comes in! VAR is like having extra eyes watching the game, but from a TV screen. Did you know? The referee can run many kilometers in just one football game, sometimes more than the players!

What is VAR? Super Slow Motion Help!

VAR stands for Video Assistant Referee. It means there are people watching the game on a video screen, very carefully. If something big happens in the game, like a goal or a foul, the VAR team can look at it again. They use slow motion and different camera angles. They want to make sure the referee made the right call. Imagine you are playing with your friends, and someone says, "Was that a goal?" You might watch a video on a phone to check. VAR is like that, but for professional football! Did you know? VAR was first used in a big tournament in 2016, so it's quite new!

The Longest Wait Ever!

Well, at a very important AFCON final game, something happened that made everyone wait and wait and wait! The referee made a decision, but then the VAR team wanted to check it. They wanted to be super sure about what happened. So, the game stopped. Everyone in the stadium, and everyone watching on TV, just waited. And waited. And waited some more! It was like waiting for a very slow bus, but for a very important football decision! Did you know? Football matches usually have two halves of 45 minutes each, but with stoppages, they often last more than 90 minutes in total!

What Happened During the Wait?

The players stood around, looking confused. The coaches paced up and down. The fans in the stadium made lots of noise, wondering what was going on. It felt like time had stopped! The VAR people were watching the video over and over again, from many different angles, trying to see everything perfectly. They were looking at something important – a goal, or maybe a penalty. These are very big moments that can change the whole game! They wanted to be absolutely sure. Did you know? A penalty kick is when a player gets to shoot the ball from a special spot, with only the goalkeeper to stop them. It's a great chance to score!

Oops! A Surprise Decision!

Finally, after a very, very long time, the referee went to look at the screen on the side of the pitch himself. He watched the video. Everyone held their breath. Then, he came back onto the pitch and blew his whistle. And guess what? He changed his first decision! It was a very big surprise for everyone! Some people were happy, and some people were very sad and confused. It was a "shock decision" because not many people expected him to change it, especially after such a long wait. Did you know? Referees use special hand signals to show what their decision is, like pointing to the centre for a goal!

Why Was Everyone Surprised?

People were surprised because the wait was so long, and usually, if VAR takes that long, it means it's a very difficult decision to make. And when the referee changed his mind, it made people wonder if the first decision was very wrong, or if the VAR check was too difficult. Some people thought the VAR should have helped faster, or that the referee should have stuck with his first decision. It caused a lot of talking and head-scratching! Did you know? Sometimes, VAR can check for things like offside (when a player is too far forward) or if a foul happened inside the penalty box.

What Happened After?

After the game, people talked a lot about this long VAR check and the surprise decision. Journalists, who are like news reporters for sports, wrote many stories. Football fans discussed it everywhere – at home, in the market, on the bus! Some people were very upset, saying it wasn't fair. Other people said VAR is good because it tries to get the right decision. This talking and discussing is what we call "the fallout." It means what happened and how people felt after the big event. It made everyone think about how football rules work. Did you know? There are millions and millions of football fans all over Africa, making it one of the most popular sports!

Learning from Our Games

So, what happens next after such a big discussion? People in charge of football will look at what happened. They will think about how VAR can be used better and faster. They want to make sure everyone understands the rules and that games are fair for all the players and teams. It's like when you play a game with your friends, and maybe you change a rule to make it more fun or fair for everyone. Football leaders will learn from this to make future AFCON games even better! Did you know? Football is played in almost every country in the world, making it the most popular sport on Earth!

Let's Keep Playing Fair!

Even with all the talking and big decisions, football is still a wonderful game! It brings us all together, cheering for our teams and our continent. We hope that with clear rules and fair play, every AFCON final will be a happy celebration for Africa! So, let's keep playing, cheering, and loving our beautiful game!
summary: At a big African football final, the referee and the video helper (VAR) took a very long time to make a decision, which surprised everyone.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone wants football games to be fair and for the rules to be clear, so all the teams and fans can enjoy the game without big surprises or long waits.

originalTitle: Leader of Ecuador-based criminal group Los Lobos arrested in Mexico City
title: Leader of Ecuador-based criminal group Los Lobos arrested in Mexico City
content: Angel Esteban Aguilar is suspected of masterminding the 2023 assassination of an Ecuadorian presidential candidate.

originalTitle: Nigeria’s President Tinubu meets royals in UK state visit
title: Big News! President Tinubu Meets King Charles!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hi everyone! Get ready for some exciting news from around the world! Today, we're going to talk about a very important meeting that happened far, far away. Our wonderful President from Nigeria, President Bola Tinubu, went on a special trip. He traveled all the way to a country called the United Kingdom, or the UK for short. In the UK, President Tinubu met with a very special person: King Charles III! Can you imagine that? Our President meeting a King! It's like two very important leaders from different countries shaking hands and becoming good friends. This meeting shows how countries can be friendly and work together.

Who is President Bola Tinubu?

President Bola Tinubu is like the number one leader of our country, Nigeria. Imagine your school has a head teacher who makes sure everything runs smoothly and helps all the students. Well, President Tinubu is like the head teacher for the whole country! He helps make important decisions for all the people in Nigeria. He wants our country to be strong and happy. **Did you know?** Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! That means lots and lots of people live here! It's a land of many different cultures and languages.

Who is King Charles III?

Now, let's talk about King Charles III. He is the King of the United Kingdom. A king is a very important leader, often from a royal family that has been leaders for many, many years. Kings and queens are like a special symbol for their country. They help bring people together and celebrate important things. King Charles lives in a very famous palace called Buckingham Palace. It's like a giant, fancy house where royal families live! **Did you know?** King Charles has a crown that he wears for very special occasions! It's usually shiny and has beautiful jewels.

Why Did They Meet?

You might be wondering, why did our President travel so far to meet a King? Well, they met to talk about important things, like how Nigeria and the UK can be even better friends. When countries are friends, they often help each other. One big way they help each other is something called "trade." Trade is like when you swap a toy with your friend. You give them your toy, and they give you theirs. But for countries, it's about buying and selling bigger things! They share things they have a lot of, and buy things they need. **Did you know?** The UK is made up of four different countries: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland!

What is Trade, Really?

Let's talk more about trade! Imagine Nigeria makes yummy palm oil and beautiful handmade crafts. And imagine the UK makes cool cars or special machines. Through trade, Nigeria can sell its palm oil and crafts to the UK. And the UK can sell its cars and machines to Nigeria. It's a win-win for everyone! When countries trade, they get things they might not be able to make themselves. This helps their people have more choices and helps their economies grow. An economy is like how much money and how many jobs a country has. More trade often means more jobs and more good things for people. **Did you know?** Countries have been trading for thousands of years, even before cars and planes existed! People used to travel by camel or ship to swap goods.

Nigeria and the UK: Old Friends

The news said that King Charles wanted to show how Nigeria and the UK have "deep ties." What does "deep ties" mean? It means they have been friends for a very, very long time. Like a friendship that has lasted for many years, even decades! They have a history of working together and helping each other. Having deep ties means that even when things are tough, these countries can talk to each other and find solutions. It's like having a best friend you can always count on. **Did you know?** The capital city of Nigeria is Abuja, and it's a very modern and beautiful city!

A Glimpse of Nigeria

Nigeria is an amazing country, full of vibrant culture and incredible people. We have beautiful beaches, vast savannas, and bustling cities. Our country is famous for its music, art, and delicious food! We are very proud to be Nigerians. When our President visits other countries, he helps share the wonderful spirit of Nigeria with the world. He shows everyone what a great country we are. **Did you know?** Nigeria has over 500 different languages spoken across the country! That's a lot of ways to say "hello"!

A Peek at the UK

The United Kingdom is also a very interesting place. It's an island country, which means it's surrounded by water. It has famous cities like London, with its big clock tower called Big Ben, and famous red buses. The UK has a long history with kings and queens, and many old castles. It's good for our President to visit places like the UK so he can learn new things and also share ideas about how to make Nigeria even better. **Did you know?** London is home to the famous London Eye, a giant Ferris wheel where you can see the whole city!

What Did They Talk About?

When President Tinubu and King Charles met, they probably talked about lots of important things. They talked about making trade even better, so Nigeria and the UK can buy and sell more good things from each other. They might have talked about how to help people, or how to protect our planet. When leaders meet, they don't just chat about the weather! They discuss serious matters that can help their countries and their people live better lives. They shook hands, smiled, and showed that they want to be good partners. **Did you know?** Leaders from different countries often exchange gifts as a sign of friendship and respect during their visits!

Why Is This Important for Us?

This meeting might seem far away, but it's important for all of us, even you! When our President meets with other leaders and builds friendships, it helps make our country stronger and more peaceful. It means more opportunities for Nigerians, maybe even new jobs when you grow up, or new exciting things to buy in our shops. When countries are friends, they can work together to solve big problems like keeping the air clean or helping people who need it. It means a safer and happier world for everyone. **Did you know?** When leaders shake hands, it's a very old way to show trust and friendship!

Hooray for Friendship!

So, remember this exciting news! Our very own President Bola Tinubu met with King Charles III in the UK. They talked about friendship, helping each other, and trading wonderful things. It's a big step for Nigeria and shows that we are an important country on the world stage. Let's cheer for good leaders and strong friendships between countries! This helps make our world a better place for all the kids and grown-ups everywhere!
summary: Nigeria's President Bola Tinubu met with King Charles III in the UK to strengthen friendship and talk about trading good things between their countries.
whyMatters: This meeting helps Nigeria and the UK be good friends, which means more opportunities for people, a stronger country, and a more peaceful world for everyone.

title: Iranians burn Trump, Netanyahu effigies at warship victim rally
content: Iranians in Tehran burned effigies of Israeli PM Netanyahu and US President Trump.

title: Israel destroys river bridges in southern Lebanon

originalTitle: One killed as ski gondola crashes down mountain at Swiss resort
title: One killed as ski gondola crashes down mountain at Swiss resort
content: Dramatic footage showed the gondola flipping over several times in the snow before coming to a halt in Engelberg.

originalTitle: Liverpool ease past Galatasaray to reach quarter-finals
title: Liverpool ease past Galatasaray to reach quarter-finals
content: Liverpool beat Galatasaray 4-0 at Anfield to advance 4-1 on aggregate in the last 16 of the Champions League to set up a quarter-final tie against Paris Saint-Germain.

originalTitle: Newcastle collapse at Nou Camp as Barcelona score seven
title: Newcastle collapse at Nou Camp as Barcelona score seven
content: Newcastle undergo a second-half collapse at the Nou Camp as they lose 7-2 on the night and 8-3 on aggregate to Barcelona in the last 16 of the Champions League.
